key_findings: dmux solves worktree isolation + agent launching with a tmux-native TUI; completely human-driven, no automation or API surface
dmux gaps: no automated coordination, no CI reaction, no programmatic control, no inter-agent communication, tmux-only
git worktrees as isolation primitive is now universal — every serious 2025-2026 tool uses them
tmux remains dominant runtime but there is growing pressure for zellij/Docker/K8s support (multiple Claude Code GitHub issues)
Agent-agnostic design is the norm — Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI treated as interchangeable plug-ins
Markdown files as agent role definitions is a recurring pattern (CAO, multiclaude, NTM)
CI-as-feedback-loop is an emerging pattern: Composio AO and multiclaude use CI pass/fail to close coordination loops automatically
Notification UX is a real pain point — dmux and cmux both prominently feature attention/notification systems
NTM is the most comprehensive local control plane: adds work intelligence, Agent Mail, safety policy, REST/WS API, durable state — heavyweight but powerful
cmux (macOS native) represents the 'terminal as primitive' philosophy: no imposed workflow, composable via CLI/socket API + built-in scriptable browser
multiclaude's 'Brownian Ratchet' philosophy (redundant work is cheap; CI is the ratchet) is a novel framing for swarm coordination
Composio AO's plugin architecture (8 swappable slots) is the most extensible design found
agent-flow is a complementary visualization layer — makes agent execution visible as node graph, works alongside any orchestrator
No tool handles: cross-machine agent distribution, output aggregation/diffing across agents, token cost tracking, or native zellij support
